article_title: DHL Hits One Billion Robotic Warehouse Picks as New Integration Platform Targets 100 Sites

content_preview.title: DHL Hits One Billion Robotic Warehouse Picks as New Integration Platform Targets 100 Sites
content_preview.summary: DHL Supply Chain and Locus Robotics completed their one billionth autonomous warehouse pick, while DHL's new SOFTBOT platform enables robotic integration 12 times faster across 30 sites with plans to expand to 100.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

DHL Supply Chain, the world's largest contract logistics provider, crossed two warehouse automation milestones in March 2026. First, its fleet of Locus Robotics autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) [completed its one billionth warehouse pick](https://roboticsandautomationnews.com/2026/03/09/dhl-and-locus-robotics-reach-one-billion-warehouse-picks-milestone/99384/) across more than 40 global fulfillment centers. Then, in a separate announcement on March 17, DHL revealed that SVT Robotics'...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid news article covering two related DHL warehouse automation milestones. Claims are well-sourced against both industry publication (Robotics and Automation News) and DHL's own press release. Key statistics — one billion picks, 12x faster integration, 8,000 robots, 30-to-180% productivity gains — all verified against primary sources. The article effectively contextualizes within the broader warehouse robotics market.
concerns: Three of five sources are not on the allowlist, though all are credible (DHL corporate press release and a respected industry trade publication)
The CNBC source URL for shipping automation contains a typo ('auotmation' instead of 'automation') but resolves correctly
recommendations: Add roboticsandautomationnews.com and group.dhl.com to the source allowlist as they are legitimate trade and corporate primary sources
